content.full_text:
Farn Porrucar Pnacucrs CouurssroN
P.O. Box 80'l . 428 J Street . Sacramenro, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
May 4,2000
Sue Summersett, MPH, RD, CFP
Correctional Food Manager
Department of the Youth Authority
Northern California Youth Correctional Center
7650 S. Newcastle Road
Post Offrce Box 213004
Stockton, California 95213-9004
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-00-087
Dear Ms. Summersett:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the statement of economic
interests disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act').r
QUESTTONS
1. Based on your disclosure category in the Department of the Youth Authority's conflict
of interest code, is the American Correctional Food Service Association ('ACFSA') a reportable
source of income or gifts to you?
2. If ACFSA is a reportable source of income or gifts to you, how is it reported on your
statement of economic interests, Form 700?
I Government Code sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless
otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, sections I 8000- I 8995.
All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Califomia Code of Regulations.


File No. A-00-087
Page2
CONCLUSIONS
l. Based on your disclosure category in the Department of the Youth Authority's conflict
of interest qode, ACFSA is neither a reportable source of income or gifts to you.
2. Because the ACFSA is not a reportable source of income or gifts to you, you need not
disclose any payments made to you by ACFSA on your statement of economic interests.
F'ACTS2
You are a Correctional Food Manager working for the Department of the Youth
Authority ("Department") and are required to file Form 700 each year disclosing your economic
interests. As a food manager, you purchase food, equipment, and services for the Department.
The disclosure category assigned to your position in the conflict of interest code of the State
Youth Authority states:
"Designated employees assigned to this disclosure category must report all
investments in and sources of income3 from business entities of the type which
provide services, supplies, materials, machinery, or equipment to the designated
employee's branch."
You are also a member of ACFSA; a national non-profit organrzation exempt from
federal income ta:r under section 501(c)(6) of the Intemal Revenue Code. ACFSA is a member
organization of the Coalition of Non-Commercial Food Service Associations, which includes the
American Dietetic Association, American School Food Service Association, and the American
Society for Hospital Food Service Administrators, among others. ACFSA members are food
service professionals employed in correction facilities and agencies within federal, state, and
municipal prison/j ail systems.
The objectives of the ACFSA are to:
Support and encourage compliance with American Correctional Association
Standards for Food Service Accreditation;
2 You provided additional facts regarding ACFSA in our telephone conversations of April 27 ,2000, and
May 1,2000.
3 Please note that uincome" is defined in section 82030(a) to include all forms of payment received by a
public official, including reimbursement of expenses, per diem payments, and "gifts."
a


File No. A-00-087
Page 3
Provide the opportunity for food service personnel working in corrections to
qualiff for "Certified Food Service Professional" status, in accordance with the
certification process adopted by the ACFSA;
Provide approved food service training courses;
Provide a means of networking among the membership that will increase
awareness of current issues and information relevant to their work;
Conduct or sponsor correctional food service conferences, forums, seminars, and
other meetings on the national, regional, state, and local levels;
Publish and distribute joumals and other professional materials concerning
various types of correctional services;
Recognize those professional members who demonstrate a high level of
competence and professionalism in the correctional food.service industry;
Offer leadership opportunities on Board and committees to enhance personal and
professional growth;
Utilize every method to strengthen and increase recognition of correctional food
service as a profession and contribute to the increasing professionalism of
correctional personnel.
ACFSA receives income from many sources including donations from competing
vendors in food services throughout the country. These donations account for 6.7Yoof ACFSA's
total gross revenues for 1999. 3.9Yo of the donations were in support of the two conferences held
during the year. The other 2.3o/o of the donations went toward continuing education scholarships
for active members. Scholarships assist members with continued formal education, training
classes and attending ACFSA sponsored conferences. Scholarships are distributed using strictly
established criteria, and payment is made by reimbursement following successful completion.
The vendors who make donations to ACFSA are not involved in the selection process and are not
informed of which members are picked for scholarships.
You have been actively involved with ACFSA for five years as a board member of the
California Chapter. The California Chapter holds ayearly training conference and trade show,
and sponsors regional training meetings within the state. As a working board member, you
receive travel and room expenses as necessary to participate in your business meetings and
training conferences. This is common practice throughout the country, and at the national level
of ACFSA. In addition, you recently received a continuing education scholarship to attend the
Intemational Conference in Tennessee in August 2000. The reimbursement for you to attend this
a
a
a
a
a


File No. ,4.-00-087
Page 4
conference will not exceed $600. As a committee chairperson for the national organization as
well as a member of the California Chapter, you will be speaking to a committee on topics
regarding division development at this conference. The conference will consist of meetings and
educational seminars. The procedure is for you to pay for your tavel, hotel room, food, and the
$140 conference fee out of pocket and then ACFSA will reimburse you up to the amount of $600
for your total expenses.
AI\ALYSIS
As an employee of a state govemmental agency, you are a public official subject to the
provisions of the Act. (Section 82048.) The Act requires all public officials designated in their
agency's conflict of interest code to file a Form 700, statement of economic interests ("SEI"),
each year disclosing investments, real property interests, and income and gifts that they have
received during the year. (Section 87200 et seq.) The Act also prohibits any designated
employee of a state government agency from accepting gifts from any single source in any
calendar year with a total value of more than $3004 if the employee would be required to report
the receipt of income or gifts from that source on his or her statement of economic interests.
(Section 89503(c).)
Travel Payments Provided By ACFSA
Under the Act, when a third party pays for a public official's travel, the payments are
generally considered either income or (most commonly) a gift to the public official. For this
reason, the SEI includes Schedule F, titled "Income/Gifts - Travel Payments, Advances, and
Reimbursements," on which public officials disclose reportable payments for travel that they
have received during the year, whether the payment was a gift or income, the name and address
of the entity that paid for the travel, the amount of the payment, and the date.
According to your disclosure category, you must disclose gifts and income from business
entities of the type which provide services, supplies, materials, machinery, or equipment to the
designated employee's branch. Section 82005 defines "business entity" as any organization or
enterprise operated for profit. You have stated in your letter that ACFSA is a national non-profit
organization exempt from federal income tax under Internal Revenue Code section 501(c)(6).
Since payments from ACFSA do not fit this description, you are neither required to disclose the
gifts on your SEI nor bound by the $300 gift limit in section 89503 as it pertains to gifts from
ACFSA.
a Ttre gift limit amount is adjusted biennially by the Commission to reflect changes in the Consumer Price
Index. As of January 1,1999, the amount is $300. (Section 87103(e); Regulation 18940.2.)


File No. 4-00-087
Page 5
Scholarship P ayment from AC FSA
The scholarship payments from ACFSA must be analyzed slightly differently from the
payments for your travel to board meetings since the scholarship payments are partly funded by
vendors. Because the scholarship is awarded only to government offrcials, it probably would be
considered a gift under the Act. (See section 82028(a).) An entity is the source of a gift if the
entity makes a gift to an official and is not acting as an intermediary. (Regulation 1S9a5(a).) If
an entity makes a payment to a third party and, in fact, directs and controls the use of the
payment to make a gift to a clearly identified public official, the entity is the source of the gift to
the official. (Regulation 18945(a)(1); also see Eisman Advice Letter, No. ,4.-98-072, Martinez
Advice Letter, No. .{-96-198, Gualco Advice Letter, No. 4-94-227, and Micheli Advice Letter,
No. A-94-136.)
In your situation, the vendors do not know which officials will be selected for
scholarships, so the true source of the gift is ACFSA and not the vendors. Because, as we
established above, ACFSA is a non-reportable source, you may accept the entire scholarship
without violating the Act and without having to report it on your SEI.
Conflict of Interest
Please note, however, that although the scholarship will not be reportable, you may
nonetheless have a potential conflict of interest in any future governmental decision which will
have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on ACFSA. This is because, under
sections 87100 and 87103, a public official is prohibited from making, participating in making,
or using his or her official position to influence any govemmental decision if the decision will
have a reasonably foreseeable financial effect on specified financial interests of the official
including a source of gifts of over $300 in the prior 12 months.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel
Cda aZ €
Qlrut <-a / da
By: Adrianne Korchmaros
Political Reform Consultant
cc: Deanna Kaihara, Filing Offrcer for Department of the Youth Authority

As an employee of a state govemmental agency, you are a public official subject to the
provisions of the Act. (Section 82048.) The Act requires all public officials designated in their
agency's conflict of interest code to file a Form 700, statement of economic interests ("SEI"),
each year disclosing investments, real property interests, and income and gifts that they have
received during the year. (Section 87200 et seq.) The Act also prohibits any designated

the receipt of income or gifts from that source on his or her statement of economic interests.
(Section 89503(c).)

generally considered either income or (most commonly) a gift to the public official. For this

gifts on your SEI nor bound by the $300 gift limit in section 89503 as it pertains to gifts from
ACFSA.
a Ttre gift limit amount is adjusted biennially by the Commission to reflect changes in the Consumer Price
Index. As of January 1,1999, the amount is $300. (Section 87103(e); Regulation 18940.2.)

The scholarship payments from ACFSA must be analyzed slightly differently from the

payment to make a gift to a clearly identified public official, the entity is the source of the gift to

In your situation, the vendors do not know which officials will be selected for
